item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting concept and I really like the execution.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.wikidot:authorName: z e a l o u s
item.content:encoded:
A thoroughly interesting read, with plenty of content, and a really good concept behind it all.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thoroughly enjoyed this, thank you for writing it!

"I’ll never forget seeing that thing come over the horizon." ("Capt. K was administered a Class-B amnestic following this interview.")

That got an honest belly-laugh out of me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really good, dude. It's always great seeing a well-written Safe, because so many people assume it has to be Keter or Thaumiel to stand out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gave me goosebumps reading the last log. The way the captain described the…"thing" was incredible. I love it when seemingly simple SCPs cause something absolutely nuts. My favorite part has got to be when the captain said,

"I knew how those Church assholes could worship a fucking machine."

Brilliant. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have a theory- more of a hunch, really- that it probably wasn't strong wind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does it use crafts that crash for non-combat reasons? Because if so the thing at the end might have just been taken down because of mechanical failure or strong wind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You're right, that line is confusing. I'll revise that and remove some unnecessary date redaction.

Thanks for the feedback.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this concept, but I have a couple points to raise. There's some simple number and date redaction throughout that seems unnecessary (the date of the Iraqi Freedom crash, the number of people extracted in the last interview). You also have some it's/its confusion throughout, remember that "it's" expands to "it is" and "its" is the possessive word.

I also think the way that the anomaly is triggered - that is, how aircraft are chosen for the "pool" of helicopters - is not explained well, and anyway doesn't make a lot of sense since there is time wankery going on. Quoting:

SCP-3539-1’s appearance will be identical to that of the helicopter that suffered a crash resulting in total crew loss nearest to SCP-3539 at the time of transmission.

This is clunkily constructed, and also not true if it's pulling helis from the future. Consider something like:

SCP-3539-1 appears identical to a helicopter that has crashed, resulting in total crew loss. SCP-3539 tends to select recently crashed aircraft, though exceptions have been made (see Addendum 3539.1).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The skip's picture is from Wikimedia, here.

Thanks for reading. Regardless of quality, it was fun to write.
